findings.message: New `redis-exporter` service is added to the prod observability stack, but `infra/observability/prometheus/prometheus.yml` has no `redis` scrape job (it scrapes only app, prometheus, otel-collector, postgres). The exporter exposes metrics on :9121 that nothing collects, so Redis remains effectively unobserved in prod despite the new service.
findings.suggestion:
Add a scrape_config to prometheus.yml, e.g.
  - job_name: redis
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["redis-exporter:9121"]

findings.message: The new `redis-exporter` (and the Redis surface it monitors) has no alert rule. infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/ contains alertmanager/otel_collector/postgres/prometheus/slo rules but nothing for redis (e.g. redis down, exporter scrape failure, memory/eviction). Redis is a prod runtime dependency (redis-exporter depends_on: redis) being instrumented with no alerting wired up.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml with at least a RedisDown / RedisExporterScrapeFailed rule routed through the existing Alertmanager receiver, mirroring postgres_alerts.yml.

findings.message: No Grafana dashboard accompanies the new redis-exporter. infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/ has otel-collector/postgres/prometheus/slo JSON but no redis dashboard, so the collected Redis metrics (once scraped) have no panel. This breaks the alert↔dashboard parity maintained for every other scrape target.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/redis.json with panels for the key redis_exporter series (up, memory, connected clients, evictions), consistent with postgres.json.
